mechanism: P-wave (compressional wave) generated by fault rupture traveling through rock at 5-7 km/second, converting partially to acoustic energy at the surface as a low-frequency rolling rumble. The first acoustic arrival of a seismic event.
status: Third entry in GEOPHYSICS ARC. Engine enters seismic territory. Cascadia Subduction Zone, Seattle Fault, intraslab earthquakes. E=5 for the P-wave rumble that precedes damaging shaking.

cascadia_subduction_zone.plates: Juan de Fuca, Explorer, Gorda subducting beneath North American
evidence: Tree-ring analysis of drowned coastal forests
Japanese historical records of Pacific-crossing tsunami

seattle_fault_zone.effects: Puget Sound tsunami, coastal land dropped into sound

nisqually_2001.type: Deep intraslab (within subducting Juan de Fuca slab)

p_wave_physics.propagation: Particle motion in same direction as wave propagation
p_wave_physics.acoustic_conversion: P-wave energy converts partially to acoustic energy at surface: low-frequency rolling rumble
p_wave_physics.frequency_range_hz: Below 20 Hz primarily; some energy to 20-100 Hz depending on magnitude and distance
p_wave_physics.duration_seconds: Seconds to 10 seconds at moderate distance
p_wave_physics.qualitative_description: Low, brief boom or rumble; directional; seems to come from below and move horizontally
p_wave_physics.shakealert_connection: ShakeAlert early warning system detects P-wave automatically; broadcasts alert before S-wave/surface wave shaking arrives. Seattle warning window for full CSZ rupture: 30-90 seconds.

theory_nodes.concept: Cascadia Subduction Zone megathrust threat. 41 events in 10,000 years. Last full rupture January 26, 1700 (M~9.0). 326 years ago. Average recurrence 200-500 years. The current gap is within the documented range. The P-wave rumble at degree 263 is the first acoustic signal of the event that will eventually occur. Question is when, not if.
theory_nodes.description: CSZ megathrust: geological certainty at human time scales.

theory_nodes.concept: P-wave as announcement. The P-wave arrives before the S-wave and surface waves by seconds to tens of seconds depending on distance. E=5 is for the announcement, not the event. The announcement is real (ground compression, acoustic energy in air) but not the full intensity of the main shaking (which would be E=7-9 depending on magnitude). ShakeAlert uses this window.
theory_nodes.description: P-wave as the announcement of the seismic event; the basis for early warning systems.

theory_nodes.concept: Nisqually (2001) as the modern reference event. M6.8, 52 km depth, intraslab. Felt BC to northern California. $2 billion damage, 400 injuries, 1 death. P-wave precursor experienced by many residents as 5-10 second rumble before shaking. Accelerated seismic retrofitting in Washington State. The reference point for public understanding of PNW earthquake.
theory_nodes.description: 2001 Nisqually: the modern PNW earthquake reference.

theory_nodes.concept: Geophysics Arc energy descent: E=7 (lahar, 262) to E=5 (P-wave, 263) to E=2 (submarine canyon, 264). The arc descends in energy as it descends in depth and moves from catastrophic events to continuous background sounds. The underground machinery operates across a wide range of acoustic scales; the arc documents the full range.
theory_nodes.description: Geophysics Arc energy descent: catastrophic to continuous.

cross_references.relationship: GEOPHYSICS ARC continues: volcanic (262, E=7) to seismic (263, E=5). Both are geophysical events producing deep ground vibration; different mechanisms.
GEOPHYSICS ARC descends: seismic episodic (263, E=5) to hydrological continuous (264, E=2). The arc reaches its quietest entry.

s36_pair.pairing_note: E=5 + E=7 = E=12. Both travel through structure: Martsch's guitar lines follow complex harmonic structures; P-waves follow rock strata. Energy propagating through a structured medium.
